reviews.review_text: Finally a truly horrifying game that elicits only the worst terrors of which I can conceive (talking to women)

There is definitely more than meets the eye with MiSide, don't be deterred by the art style this game can be pretty unsettling. It's an interesting mix of cutness with a side of psychological horror. The story hits different with how rampant AI and loneliness is these days, kinda disturbing when you really think about. The ending though leaves alot to be desired, I thought since it's short and the base ending is so meh there must be multiple endings but apparently that's it. Gameplay wise it's a super simple walking sim but with some decent mini games and puzzles on top. I wish there were more spooky segments, what's there is good just not enough of it. The sound effects and music are top notch and the menus are clean, some of the animations are really great as well. The game just feels way too much like it's on rails most the time super linear. The writing and story is good enough to carry though, has some good jump scares and creepyness going for it. It's something different in the indie horror space for sure, creative devs. I didn't experience any glitches or performance issues, it supports ultrawide ect. Feels like a well polished game. It's a unique well crafted horror game amongst an abundance of generic asset flips on Steam. 7.5/10

Best 6.8 hours I’ve spent in my life. Seriously, this game is worth every single dollar.

The story was amazing and kept surprising me the whole time, and some of the jump scares genuinely caught me off guard. This is currently one of my  favorite horror games of all time!

The characters were great, the game design was super well made, and there were SO many cool easter eggs hidden throughout the game. (Shoutout to the music team for making insanely catchy songs btw)

I’d highly recommend jumping straight into the game without watching gameplay videos or spoilers first. Trust me, the experience is way better that way. I personally didn’t need help with the puzzles, but some of them might be tricky for some people, but you could easily find the solutions on YouTube by looking up the name of the chapter or the objective.

Easily a 10/10 game and I’d recommend it to anyone wanting to play a horror game.

Praying for you 🕯️ O Great Mita 💝
More of a experience, than a horror game. It definetely has horror elements and will keep you on your toes, but will never make you exit the game, because you Sh*t your pants. UwU.

TL;DR: Great story but it's too short and underdelivered.

I know the other reviews state that the game is a great play, but I am going to give you my own thoughts as to why you'd be better off saving your money.
1. The game is about 4 to 5 hours long, and that's if you 100% everything. You really don't get enough time to get invested in the story, because it's very basic. Understandable, but not emotionally investable.
2. It is now June of 2026, the 2nd year anniversary is this December. We were promised more content and we have yet to receive it.
3. I don't think because the game has a few horror aspects that makes it "Psychological Horror", because the horror aspects in the game are (no offense) but lazily done and are expected. Rarely have I ever been spooked while playing this.
4. Besides the promised content, where is peaceful mode? "First half of 2026" and it is now the first half of 2026 and we have yet to receive an update as to what progress they are making.
5. The game seems abandoned by the devs. There is no communication at all as to what they are doing with this game. Just radio silence.

Ultimately, the game has potential, but the devs don't seem to think so. The story is short but rushed in some areas, the horror is a bad lackluster attempt at trying to scare the player, and there is no word as to whether or not this game is actually being worked on. I think there are some good features like the minigames, but I would save your money until the devs actually give us something worthwhile.

lowkey I had this game for a weeks and never played it. This game was weird at first but for me playing this in one sitting for 4 hours. The game is scary in few parts and there is sexual stuff but not much, 8.5/10 game

This is an absolutely spectacular game! I ended up spending the entire weekend playing it and I would definitely recommend it to anyone who loves adult horror with a splash of comedy and drama! 11 out of 10. And I saw that they are still coming out with new updates as of now and for the diehard fans, you will have so much more to enjoy!

I went into this game with low expectations. I came out with a very good time. This game is honestly really good. It has a lot of cute things, and a lot of very creepy things. I wasn't expecting to actually enjoy this as much as I did. 10/10 game. I would recommend going into it blind!

My favorite parts of the game were honestly the environments and QoL features (I think that's what they're called). I am SO glad the game allows you to keep going even if you "die" and I am glad that I can load different chapters (versions) to go back and check stuff I might've missed. I get spooked pretty easily so I found the game pretty scary tbh - BUT, I think most people would get through this with no problem. Most of the horror is atmospheric so it doesn't rely on jumpscares to set the mood.

One aspect of the game I really liked was the shift between different "game styles" (idk how to explain without revealing too much) - it was super cool!

Overall I quite liked the story too, was a fun experience! :D

Fantastic game, great story, great pacing, and just a good and polished game in general. The sheer amount of attention to detail also amazes me, you should look closely at the skins of Mita, the attention to detail is fantastic. The world building is also really good, so if you like good story like games with a bit of horror, i suggest buying this game.

MiSide é um jogo incrível e dá para perceber claramente o quanto os desenvolvedores se dedicaram em cada detalhe do projeto a história é muito bem construída e consegue prender a atenção do jogador do começo ao fim principalmente pelos mistérios e pela atmosfera diferente que o jogo transmite as variações da Mita também são muito interessantes fazendo com que cada uma seja única assim criando vários momentos marcantes

Os gráficos são muito bem feitos principalmente considerando o estilo do jogo os cenários possuem bastante personalidade e conseguem criar uma ambientação que combina perfeitamente com a proposta do jogo além disso a trilha sonora é outro ponto extremamente forte porque consegue transmitir tensão emoção e até tranquilidade em certos momentos a soundtrack realmente combina com cada situação do jogo e deixa a experiência ainda melhor

Outro aspecto muito interessante é a platina do jogo ela é equilibrada e divertida de fazer  já que não é algo impossível ou exageradamente difícil mas também não é tão fácil a ponto de perder a graça

No geral MiSide é uma experiência muito marcante e diferenciada é um jogo que consegue unir uma boa narrativa gráficos bonitos trilha sonora excelente e uma jogabilidade divertida em um único pacote Por tudo isso recomendo bastante.

There will be a lot of people who tell you this game is comparable to Doki Doki Literature Club. Unfortunately, the similarities begin and end with "Character knows this is a video game". The rest is much more akin to a strange Rick and Morty episode, or in some places, Five Nights at Freddy's. 

Ultimately, the game for what it is, is fine. The problem is that it's only "fine". There's nothing great about this game, and the ultimate conclusion is confusing, and unfinished. 

"Unfinished" is at the core of the problem here. The game IS unfinished. It's not V 1.0 or beyond. It's an unfinished game, that is not listed as an early access, even though it technically is. There is also nothing on the store page to indicate that the game isn't finished. 

The ultimate "reward" for finishing it, is unlocking a mode for the game that does not actually exist. This is misleading advertisement, when you're charging money for a game that has not been completed. I'm not familiar with this developer's other work, and as such, I have no idea if the finished game will ever actually be a thing. Therefore, since I was mislead into buying an incomplete game, and because there's no reason to believe (after two years) that there will be a completed version, I have to give it a negative review. 

Incomplete, corny in places, no grand secrets to be found, no real reward at the end. Loose story lines that never get tied up, a story that leaves you with all questions, and no answers. No real "moral to the story" in this story centered game. An antagonist with completely unclear goals. Confusing story twists, and inconsistent characters.

Wanted to like it but it relies heavily on jump scares and linear gameplay.
AI spots you very easily or is very buggy.

The first game that REALLY made me want to go for 100% and I did it in one night. It was a somewhat short but really enjoyable journey throughout the game and was still creeping me out all the way through. The story of it had me really engaged and it was really interesting to think of theories/possibilities. It took a decently expanded upon idea and made it into this refreshing story that gives you a lot to think about. I loved every single second of this game through and I still can't wait for the peaceful mode. The only thing I wish the game had is just more to see and experience. (This is a belated review because I forgot to give one back in 2024)
Got spooked but was a really good game with great storytelling. Lot's of lore to be discovered.
I bought this game cause I was bored and also saw someone play it was peak. Would play again just waiting till they add the peacefull mode
This game was a somewhat refreshing and welcome surprise. It has enough twists and turns to keep you engaged. I think it's a solid recommend.

It was amazing to see such a game have so many different dubs across a wide variety of different languages from fans. The game has an amazing story with some mind-messing visuals that keep you guessing. The jumpscares got me multiple times and the general aspect of horror lands perfectly. The details are some of this game's biggest positives. If you want a biting thriller experience mixed with cute art, this game is a must play.

Meh, it's a 5 out of 10. Not a terrible game, but not bad enough for me to downvote it. Steam needs a "meh" vote option. The visuals are great, the audio is good, and the story is somewhat interesting, but the ending was flat and the "gameplay" was a bit dry, basically a walking simulator. All-in-all, the games not great, not bad, just meh. 

I did laugh at the line: "that's for riling up the Beavers" - wish I coulda saw that scene lol.

According to discussions and google, there are only 2 people working on miside. Peaceful mode IS (is as in is) coming, and the last development update was on March 7, 2026. Don't trust the "lazy devs" reviews.

This is one of the best horror games that I've ever played HANDS DOWN; it is not to be sat on. I love this new concept, but I will not spoil anything for any potential readers, and I highly recommend just turning your lights off, locking yourself into a room, and going into this game blind at 3:00am. *Also*, you can learn about how love works!

Yeah sure, it's short for the money, okay? I get it. (It took me around six and a half hours for a full enjoyment run--I still missed a lot of collectibles, and there are a few different endings which also add to re-playability, as well as the new Peaceful mode which others seem to be enjoying!) If you aren't into anime, it may *not* be of interest to you. But if you don't mind that part (or maybe even enjoy it), and you are an avid horror enthusiast, this is one for the books... 

I have played 50+ horror games in my lifetime and this one comes so close to my Top 5 that it took me off guard a bit with all of the pink. Hats off to AIHASTO, and please keep this up! 10/10

Great game. Even better if you go in completely blind. Doesn't overstay its welcome and has lots of fun easter eggs and references to look out for. Not sure why Mita's voice was off by default so make sure to set that to something (I had mine on Japanese as i think it used to be the default) before starting a new game. The achievements are pretty fun to go for after beating the game too with a few different endings.

Just thought it'd be cool if we had the choice of gender instead of having only male MC as an option. Please and thank you:D

I'm not a massive fun of super scary games and this didn't keep me up all night after playing but was creepy and scary enough to keep me entertained.

Pretty cool game, lots of easter eggs and side-contents along the way. I like how it allows players to immerse with the characters despite the limited time we spend with them jumping between versions. Each characters are unique and are chill to spend time with. I'd recommend the game if you're looking for a blend of horror and interactive, funny & emotional moments with the characters. The minigames are not that difficult, yet fun.

One thing I'd probably criticize is that it probably needs more content with other characters, as in more interactions/minigames/dialogues. Would be pretty cool if they add more difficulties that'd test more of our capabilities in the future, since they'll be adding more endings maybe additional challenges in each chapters? Nevertheless, it's a fun game!
Very engaging story and great replay-ability. You can tell there was genuine care put into this game.

The only downside is that the game isn't longer. Can't wait for peaceful mode to come out and really hope the developers are actually working on releasing it rather than give false promises. The atmosphere was pretty good and I always love liminal spaces

This game is a masterpiece, must I say possibly the best game ever made? I find myself intrigued by the horror and the story of what's happening, whilst exploring you find yourself meeting funny and cool characters who act as a relief giving you a sort of slice of life experience while playing minigames!! Talking about minigames this game has you playing minigames of many genres for example; shooting, racing, fighting, and who knows what else!! You may think it takes away from the horror by in my opinion it adds to it even more, making the scary moments even more scary when they do appear, this game is worth every cent plus more, Dare I say this game is 10/10 and I can tell a lot of thought went into it! I cannot wait for the peaceful mode update which will add even more to a already perfect game. SPOILER ALERT!!! (Btw my favourite moment had to be when you are walking across the bridge and Chibi Mita runs away, HILARIOUS!! and when you look to the side you can see the chair multiply, it was genuinely so funny and genius.) P.S. CAPPIE THE GOAAAATTTT

Overall would recommend. 8/10

This game is nuts. You're taken on a wild adventure where reality turns on its head while being a balanced mix of nice, endearing, and disturbing with twists and turns throughout. It's equally a feel-good anime romance and a horror thriller. I loved it. The game is extremely well made, the devs clearly very talented. I highly recommend this. Took me 5 and a half hours to play through the main questline which I did in a single sitting because I couldn't tear myself away from it. There's another second full alternative play-through coming in a future update that I'm looking forward to as well (unless you're reading this in the future in which case it might already be added.)

The game itself is decent with psychologicial horror and a commentary on escapism which i am not qualified to breakdown. Personally what really stuck with me are the characters and the OST which i've listened to countless times, and the game genuinely has some of my favourite instrumental tracks.

Some find it underwhelming and too short (took me ~6 hours to finish) which i can understand, but if it looks like something you'd like give it a try.

For me, this has been one of the most creative, fun, interesting, and sometimes terrifying game/VN I have played in a long time. What else can I say that hasn't already been said by thousands of others? I am hoping and praying that peaceful mode will be just as good.

If you’re looking for a game that will hook you instantly, mess with your head, and leave you wondering what’s real, MiSide is it. Released in December 2024 by the tiny two-person studio AIHASTO, it exploded on Steam with an "Overwhelmingly Positive" rating from over 132,000 reviews . It’s a wild ride that blends cute anime aesthetics with genuine psychological horror, all wrapped in a meta-narrative about escapism and loneliness.

The Good The game is a masterclass in atmosphere. You start by caring for a virtual girl named Mita in what feels like a harmless mobile Tamagotchi. But as the story progresses, the cute facade cracks, revealing something much darker. The horror isn’t just about jump scares; it’s about the creeping realization that the game world is broken and that Mita’s obsession with you is dangerous. The contrast between the kawaii visuals and the unsettling events is chilling .

The gameplay variety is another huge win. It constantly shifts gears from a dating sim to a horror adventure, throwing in mini-games like a Quake-style shooter, Tetris, and racing segments . The puzzles are generally straightforward, which keeps the pace moving without frustrating you.

My Favorite Moment: Cappie Without a doubt, the highlight for me was Cappie. The dance sequence with her was an absolute blast. It was a perfect break from the tension, letting you just vibe with the character and enjoy the upbeat energy. It’s rare to find a horror game that balances its scares with moments of genuine fun like that.

The Not-So-Good (The Short Length) The only real downside is how quickly it ends. You can blast through the main story in about 4 hours . For a game this engaging, it feels a tiny bit too short. You’ll find yourself wanting more of that world and those characters right as the credits roll. While there are collectibles and secrets to encourage a second playthrough, the main narrative wraps up faster than you’d expect.

Verdict MiSide is a modern indie gem that punches way above its weight. It’s visually stunning, technically solid, and tells a story that will stick with you long after you finish. Yes, it’s short, but the impact it leaves is massive. If you enjoy psychological horror with a twist of anime charm, this is a must-play.

Rating: 8.5/10
(Would be a 10 if it had a bit more content!)
